info.title: ID Document and Biometric Verification: Assure API
info.description:
Introduction to the Assure API

The Assure API is a RESTful service that you can use to perform electronic identity verification (eIDV) of your customers during onboarding. This means you can scan and verify identity documents using one or more of the supported eIDV services. It also allows to obtain identity information from eID methods and to match and corroborate the data from these sources. The purpose of the Assure API is to provide a unique and simple way of integrating these identification services, working both in a browser and native mobile contexts and supporting asynchronous operations.

This service creates a new process in the dossier, using a given provider. You use this service in most flows, unless you integrate through the eIDV alternative flow for the web.
The request can accept several parameters. However, which parameters to use, depends on the provider and type of process. For more details, see the parameter descriptions below.
After you have created the process, you cannot change the information you sent in its request. This means, if you need to change any of the information sent in its parameter, you must create a new process. Alternatively, you can delete the old process.

delete.description:
This service deletes all information about a process.

Warning: This is a destructive operation since you cannot do a rollback afterwards.

Note: For Onfido, you are not allowed to delete a process if it is still in the `processing` state (for more details about process statuses, see the Get process page in the integration documentation).

These are the steps for deleting a process:
  • Before you delete, you should wait until the verification is finished, and then save all the information by using the Get process and Download full result services.
  • After saving, use this 'Delete process' service (and later also Delete dossier if you are finished with that end-user’s identity verification).
  • Once the delete operation has finished, the provider of the process will receive a request to delete the information on their side. The information will either be immediately deleted or soft-deleted, i.e., it will be marked for deletion and hard-deleted after X days (decided by each provider).

get.description:
Use this service to return images from the process.

Note: The images were either uploaded to the process directly through the Set images endpoint or by using the provider’s SDK to capture and upload them.

Supported depiction types are `front` and `back` images of the ID document, `selfie` images of the end-user, and `portrait`images extracted from the ID document (portrait is available only for some providers).

Supported file formats are jpg/png.

For more information, see the Images section in the integration documentation.

post.description:
Use this service to add images to the process, which in turn will upload images directly to a provider (i.e. not using their SDK to capture and upload the images).

Note: Direct upload using Assure is only available for Onfido, Signicat Paper and FaceTec.

Supported depiction types are `front` and `back` images of the ID document, `selfie` images of the end-user, and `portrait` images extracted from the ID document (portrait is available only for some providers).

Supported file formats are jpg/png. The file must be sent using the dataURL scheme.

You can send one or more images at the same time. If the same depiction type (e.g. selfie) is sent more than once, only the last one is kept.

You are not allowed to upload images after the verification has started.

For more information, see the Images section in the integration documentation.

post.description:
This service initiates a flow for performing an identity document verification in a web context. Since this service encapsulates all providers’ Web SDKs, you do not have to integrate with the provider’s SDK yourself. When you call this service, it will:
  • Create a new service on your behalf.
  • Integrate directly with the provider's JS SDK and use it to display a web view to the end-user where they can capture and upload the ID images.
  • Request to start the verification.
When you call this service, you must provide similar request parameters as in the Create process service. For more details, see the parameter descriptions below.

This service also provides other useful features. For example, you can configure the user interface during the web flow. For more usage details about this, see the general documentation in the Start Capture flow section.

get.description: You can use this service to retrieve information about all the processes you have created the last 30 days (as long as they are not deleted). This can be useful, for example, to investigate how many processes have failed and why, to see how many processes are accepted/rejected etc.The result is presented as a list. You can filter the process list on creation date, status, offset and limit (see 'query parameters' below).

parameters.name: startCreationDate
parameters.description: The start date and time (ISO-8601 with UTC: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) for the first process in the process list. This date filter is relative to the date the process was created (the `createdAt` field of the process).

parameters.name: endCreationDate
parameters.description: The end date and time (ISO-8601 with UTC: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ) for the last process in the process list

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: This field allows you to filter the process list on one or more statuses. The default value is an empty string, which means it returns all processes with all statuses.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description:
You can enter an offset value to specify which process should be retrieved first. The default offset value is 0 (first position in the database) and maximum is 200000. This maximum number is set to avoid performance issues. If you want to display more processes than defined in 'limit', you can create several requests with a different offset:
1. Create a request for the most recent items: GET /processes?limit=20&offset=0
2. On scroll/next page, create a second request: GET /processes?limit=20&offset=20
3. On scroll/next page, create a third request: GET /processes?limit=20&offset=40

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: This is the maximum number of processes allowed in the response. The default value is 100 and maximum allowed is 2000. If you want to look at more processes than the defined limit, you must make more requests using the 'offset' parameter (see the example above).

parameters.name: order
parameters.description:
Allows to choose the order on which the processes will be returned - by descending or ascending createdAt date.
Default is descending.

400.description:
Please check the filter values:
  • startCreationDate should be <e endCreationDate
  • status must be valid.
  • offset or limit maximum value was overpassed

200.description: The list of processes was successfully retrieved.

normalization.description:
The normalization to be applied to source and target values.

  • 'none': No conversion - case sensitive scenario.
  • 'upper': Converts all characters to upper case - ignores case scenario.
  • 'clean': Converts into latin script - Following ICAO Doc 9303 Part 3 Section 6 recommendation.
  • 'cleanUpper': Apply clean an upper normalization.
  • 'initialsUpper': Converts to the first two initials from multiple words.
  • 'numeric': Converts to the 0-9 characters only.

algorithm.description:
The matching algorithm to be used.

  • 'levenshtein': String compare normalized values
    • Exact match: True if all characters match
    Partial match: True if the similarity ratio is above 90% of characters
  • 'fuzzy': String compare normalized values
    • Exact match: True if all characters match
    Partial match: True if the similarity ratio is above 90% of characters or if one of multiple words are an exact match.

sdkLoadTimeout.description: Number of seconds to wait for the VideoID SDK to load. After this time, the process status will be set to `failed`. This is a UX measure to avoid end-users getting stuck waiting (and the process stuck at `pending`) if there are problems with VideoID services.

Image to be watermarked.
Must be in dataURL format (Example: `data:image/png;base64,iVBORw....`)
The following formats are supported: png, jpg
Minimum image size allowed is 250x120 pixels.
Maximum size allowed is:
  • 4MB
  • 5000px
Note: Recommended maximum size is 1080px.

processId.description: Apply a watermark on the image from this process.

The type of image to be retrieved from the process.

processId.description: Redact the image from this process

documentCountry.description: Country of the document to be redacted (ISO 3166 Alpha 3).

fieldsToRedact.description: It indicates which field(s) should be redacted from the ID images.

redactionLevel.description: This is the size of the bounding box for the redaction functionality. 'standard' (smallest) is default.

redactedFrontImage.description: Redacted front image in dataURL format

redactedBackImage.description: Redacted back image in dataURL format

detail.description: Detail on the outcome of the redaction process
detail.example: Please check the image has enough quality and that the document provided is from the list of supported documents.

externalReference.description: Customer specific reference to be associated with an ID of a dossier.

Dossier.description: Dossiers are objects that contain end-user identity data, e.g. name, date of birth, ID document, etc. They also contain any processes that are created to verify that end-user’s identity.

dossierId.description: This ID is obtained from the "Create dossier" response body.

externalReference.description: Customer specific reference to be associated with an ID of a dossier.

createdAt.description: The date and time the dossier was created (ISO-8601 with UTC: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ).

updatedAt.description: The date and time the dossier was updated (ISO-8601 with UTC: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ).

privacy.description:
This field is used only if `processType` is `documentSelfie` or `documentVideo` and is only required if the end-user is located in the USA or unknown location.

Indicates if the end-user has read and agreed to Onfido’s Privacy Notices and Consent. If the end-user does not agree, Start Verification will fail.

countryOfResidence.description:
The country where the end-user lives (in ISO 3166 Alpha 3 format. ex: “USA”, “NOR”, “GBR”).
If this field is not provided, it will be inferred from the IP address, which may result in an incorrect value.

referrer.description:
This field is required for 'onfido' processes where the images are uploaded via JS (Web) SDK.
This is the URL of the web page where the Web SDK will be used.

mobileAppId.description:
This field is required for 'onfido' processes where the images are uploaded via iOS/Android (mobile) SDK.
This is a unique ID of the mobile app that will integrate with the provider's iOS/Android SDK. This is either a bundle ID for iOS or application ID for Android.

location.description:
This field is required for `onfido` processes where the images are uploaded via the Set Images endpoint.

The end-user’s location.

consents.description: This field is used only for `onfido` processes where the images will be uploaded using the Set Images endpoint.

requestExtraDataFromDrivingLicense.description: (Warning: This is a Beta feature. Please contact your account manager for details.) This field is available only for "onfido" processes and for specific Driving License documents. If enabled, the process result will contain more information extracted from the Driving License (ex: license category and vehicle classes).

webid.description:
This object only applies to the 'webid' provider and is required.
The information in this field must be provided at the creation of the process. It will be compared with the user information gathered during the process.

provider.description:
The provider that performs the identity document verification. This field is always required for all the providers (see `Enum` above).

For more information about supported eIDV methods from Signicat, see the overview of eIDVs in the general documentation.

processType.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatvideoid', 'onfido' or 'readid' provider.

The available values are:
  • For 'signicatvideoid':
    • `high` (default): A human agent will review the process. This is done asynchronously.
    • `substantial`: Process verification is done automatically, meaning no human agent is involved.
    • `substantialFullyAuto`: Similar to `substantial` but without any `inconclusive` status. It will automatically reach an `accepted` or `rejected` status. For more information about what each status means, see the Get process page in the integration guide.
  • For 'onfido'
    • `document`: The verification is only done on the provided identity document.
    • `documentSelfie` (default): The verification is done on the identity document and the photo of the end-user. The photo allows assessing whether the holder of the identity document is the same person as the one in the ID document (facial similarity). The photo must be taken at the time of the request.
    • `documentVideo`: The verification is done on the identity document and also a video-call while the end-user does some requested actions (e.g. “Look over your left shoulder”). The video allows assessing whether the holder of the identity document is the same person as the one on the video-call(facial similarity). It also detects liveness information.
  • For 'readid'
    • `sdk` (default)
    • `ready`
  • For 'webid'
    • `videoid`: The end-user is identified by a support agent in a live video call.
    • `accountid`: The end-user identifies themselves by capturing their ID and selfie.
  • For 'signicatpictureid'
    • `document`: The verification is only done on the provided identity document.
    • `documentSelfie` (default): The verification is done on the identity document and the photo of the end-user. The photo allows assessing whether the holder of the identity document is the same person as the one in the ID document (facial similarity). The photo must be taken at the time of the request.

documentType.description:
This field only applies to the 'onfido' or 'signicatpaper' provider.
It is required when images are uploaded via the Assure API (not via the SDK).
This field defines the document type used in the verification.
To check the available values, use the "Get Document Types" service.

documentCountry.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider and is then a required field.
It represents the country of the document that will be used in the verification (ISO 3166-1 Alpha 2), e.g. `NL`.

For passports only, it is also possible to use the `UNIV` value. This allows any ICAO compliant passport to be used in the verification. However, in this case, Signicat Paper will only extract information from the MRZ area. This is useful, for example, if you want information from a passport that is not supported by Signicat Paper and you only need information that is in the MRZ area. For a full list of documents supported by Signicat, see the Get document types endpoint.
Note: If you use the `UNIV` value, redaction will not be performed.

redact.description:
This field can only be used when the provider is 'signicatpaper')
It indicates which field(s) should be redacted from the ID images.
This will affect the 'front' and 'back' images you are able to get from the process If you request redaction, you will only be able to get those images if they were successfully redacted (i.e., if 'isFullRedaction':'true' in the Get Process response).

redactionLevel.description:
This field can only be used when the provider is 'signicatpaper'.
This is the size of the bounding box for the redaction functionality. 'standard' (smallest) is default.

eventTags.description: List of tags to be used on events API

documentId.description: Required for provider 'signicatpictureid'. Unique id for the document that will be used to identify the end-user. Get the full list of possible id document from Get Document Types.

street.description: Available only if addressLine1 and addressLine2 are empty.

streetNumber.description: Available only if addressLine1 and addressLine2 are empty.

addressLine1.description: For example, street number and street, or post box or rural route. Available only if street and streetNumber are empty.

addressLine2.description: Available only if street and streetNumber are empty.

region.description: For example, state, district, province or any local region.

mobile.description: The end-user's mobile phone number. Required for `accountid` processes only (not `videoid`).

fileName.description: File name with extension. Extension must be "pdf". Slashes ('/') are not allowed in this field.

WebIDIdDocument.description: Data about the physical ID document. Required for `accountid` processes only (not `videoid`).

authority.description: Name of the authority that issued the ID document. Might be empty.

dateOfIssue.description: Date when the ID document was issued. No time zone. Might be empty.

documentType.description: Type (or name) of the ID document, for example, passport. Only specified values are supported. Other values will be ignored.

mrz.description: Value of the "Machine Readable Zone" in the ID document. Cannot include line breaks or special characters for multi-line variants. Might be empty.

nameAtBirth.description: Maiden name as specified in the ID document.

nationality.description: The end-user's nationality (country code in ISO 3166 Alpha 3 format). Required for `accountid` processes only (not `videoid`).

preferredLanguage.description: Your preferred language as a two-letter ISO-3166-1 Alpha-2 code (optional). If specified, this will be used for web pages, emails, etc. directed to the user. Supported language codes are en, de, fr, it. Other values will be mapped to one of these.

user.description: Data about the end-user. Usually, entered by the end-user into your system.

hasProvidedConsent.description: If the user has already accepted the terms and conditions. Default is 'false'.

file.description: PDF file which must correspond to the PDF/A standard (PDF/A 1.3b recommended). Documents on QES processes are intended to be digitally signed by a user throughout the process.

title.description: Academic title of the user. Will be synchronised with the corresponding field in the ID document.

address.description: Residence or postal address, according to the ID document. Either street or addressLine1 is required. Street and streetNumber may be used to infer the required part of the address. For international addresses, addressLine1 and addressLine2 may be used to infer the required part of the address.

WebIDUserActionParameters.description: Parameters the WebID service needs for the end-user flow.

redirectUrl.description: URL to redirect the user after a successful identification (or signature) (only in web browsers).

redirectDeclineUrl.description: URL to redirect the user after an unsuccessful identification (only in web browsers).

redirectMismatchUrl.description: URL to redirect the user after a successful identification, even if the user data has changed during the  identification (only in web browsers).

redirectSkipQesUrl.description: URL to redirect the user when they click the Cancel button on the contract signing page.

redirectCancelUrl.description: URL to redirect the user when they manually cancel the identification process.

redirectTime.description: (When testing in sandbox, this needs to be > 0 https://developer.signicat.com/docs/id-document-and-biometric-verification/provider-specific-integrations/webid/#test-webid) Time in seconds to delay redirect to one of the redirect URLs. If this is 0, users will be redirected directly to those URLs. Otherwise, a success page will be shown after successful identification and redirect occurs after the specified delay.

clientName.description: Name of client or brand customer if the user-action is attributed to a sub-customer. May be shown to a support agent.

productType.description: The product type shown to the support agent to help detect fraud. The value should only be one single product type or category, and it should be either in German or English. Examples: "Account Opening", "Loan Application", "Credit Card", "Financing", "SIM Card Activation", "Age Verification", "Contract".

processId.description: processId is obtained from the "Create process" response body.

provider.description: The ID of one of the supported providers.

processType.description: The process type created from the provider.

status.description: The current status of the the identity document verification process. For more information about what each status means, see the Get process page in the integration documentation.

authorization.description: The authorization token that is necessary for uploading images using the provider's SDK. This is not necessary if the images are uploaded directly through the API, using the Set Images service.

providerApiUrl.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatvideoid' or 'readid' provider.

For signicatvideoid:
This is the URL for Signicat Video ID's API. Use this URL to load Signicat Video ID's SDK in your native/web apps.
In native apps, just use this URL directly to load the iOS/Android SDK.
In web apps you must load the JS SDK by adding the following to the URL:
  • For default "processType":"high", for "processType":"substantial" and for "processType":"substantialFullyAuto", you must append "/js/videoid.js". Example: "https://etrust-sandbox.electronicid.eu/v2/js/videoid.js"
For 'readid':
This is the URL for ReadID's API. Use this URL in the SDK configuration.

universalLink.description: This field only applies to the 'readid' provider with `processType` set to `ready`. This link is presented to the end-user (instead of `base64QRCode`) when they are using a mobile device. You must append a callback query parameter to the universal link, like in this example: `universallink&callback=//redirectuserhere.com`Selecting this link, redirects the end-user to the ReadID Ready app. After the ReadID Ready identification process is completed, the end-user is redirected to the link provided as the callback parameter.

This object contains necessary information to validate the liveness of a person. The FaceTec SDK provides the returned values of these liveness checks. The values are matched against `imageDepiction`.

It defines the image depiction in the process that is used to compare against the images captured in `facetecEnrollment` (see 'Set Images to the process' to see which depictions are valid for 'facetec' processes).

uiProfile.description:
This is the graphical profile used when loading the SDK. This graphical profile impacts the frontend layout (e.g. font, colour) and the end-user workflow (e.g. available documents, auto capture, glare detection). For more details about the uiProfile and configurations, please see the general documentation Capture configurations (uiProfile). If a uiProfile is not set, a default value will be used.

To create a uiProfile configuration use the Create capture configuration endpoint.

To get all available configurations use the Get all configurations endpoint.

uiProfileJSON.description: This is JSON format of the graphical profile used when loading the SDK. Attention this will override configuration properties defined in the uiProfile.For more details about the uiProfile and configurations, please see the general documentation Capture configurations (uiProfile).

uiLanguage.description: Sets the initial language for the user interface. Default value is 'en' (English). For details about possible values, see the Translations section in the integration documentation.

smsSender.description: Name of the sender that will appear in the text message if the end-user chooses to capture the images with their mobile phone (mobile handover). This field must either be numeric and consist of up to 15 digits ([0-9]), or be alphanumeric and consist of 1 to 11 characters in the range [A-Za-z0-9]. Default value is 'Signicat'.

smsMessage.description: The text that will appear in the text message. This message is prepended to the URL where the end-user must click to continue the verification process on their mobile phone, if they decided to do a mobile handover (see 'smsSender'). This message must follow the regex expression: `^[A-Za-z0-9-.:s]{1,200}$`Thus, it cannot exceed 200 characters. Default value is 'To continue, please click here:'

defaultCountry.description: The default country (ISO 3166 Alpha 3) to be used during the flow. This country is selected when there is a list of countries. Setting `defaultCountry` here in the request will override whatever  defaultCountry is set in the capture configuration.

provider.description: The provider that performs the identity document verification. This field is always required for all the providers (see `Enum` above).

processType.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatvideoid', 'onfido' or 'readid' provider. It is optional for 'signicatvideoid' and 'onfido' and required for 'readid'.

The available values are:
  • For 'signicatvideoid':
    • `high` (default): A human agent will review the process. This is done asynchronously.
    • `substantial`: Process verification is done automatically, meaning no human agent is involved.
    • `substantialFullyAuto`: Similar to `substantial` but without any `inconclusive` status. It will automatically reach an `accepted` or `rejected` status. For more information about what each status means, see the Get process page in the integration guide.
  • For 'onfido'
    • `document`: The verification is only done on the provided identity document.
    • `documentSelfie` (default): The verification is done on the identity document and the photo of the end-user. The photo allows assessing whether the holder of the identity document is the same person as the one in the ID document (facial similarity). The photo must be taken at the time of the request.
    • `documentVideo`: The verification is done on the identity document and also a video-call while the end-user does some requested actions (e.g. “Look over your left shoulder”). The video allows assessing whether the holder of the identity document is the same person as the one on the video-call(facial similarity). It also detects liveness information.
  • For 'readid'
    • `ready` (required)
  • For 'webid'
    • `accountid`
    • `videoid`

redact.description:
This field can only be used when the provider is 'signicatpaper')
It indicates which field(s) should be redacted from the ID images.
This will affect the 'front' and 'back' images you are able to get from the process If you request redaction, you will only be able to get those images if they were successfully redacted (i.e., if 'isFullRedaction':'true' in the Get Process response).

requestExtraDataFromDrivingLicense.description: (Warning: This is a Beta feature. Please contact your account manager for details.) This field is available only for "onfido" processes and for specific Driving License documents. If enabled, the process result will contain more information extracted from the Driving License (ex: license category and vehicle classes).

webid.description:
This object only applies to the 'webid' provider and is required.
The information in this field must be provided at the creation of the process. It will be compared with the user information gathered during the process.

providers.description: This is a list (array) of providers that perform the verification. Please, use only one provider per request.

sdk.description:
Identifies which SDK will be used to capture and upload the images. The available values are:
  • `native`: Uses the provider's native SDK. This is default when the provider is `eid`, `readid`or `onfido`.
  • `capture`: Uses Signicat's SDK. This is default when the provider is `signicatpaper`.
See also the general documentation, Using the SDKs available in the Capture service.

redirectUrl.description: Contains the URL where the end-user will be redirected to when the image upload is finished (either successfully, failed or canceled).

requestDomain.description: This specifies the domain you want to use for this specific artifact. The domain will be visible in the end-user's browser. This domain needs to be correctly configured on your account!

It corresponds to different things in each provider: Onfido: applicantId, Signicat VideoID: videoId, ReadID: sessionId (only after the process is finished. Before that it's the same as processId), Signicat Paper: requestId, Facetec: n/a, WebID: actionId, Signicat PictureID: transactionId.

statusList.description: This field allows you to filter the process list on one or more statuses. The default value is an empty string, which means it returns all processes with all statuses.

offset.description:
You can enter an offset value to specify which process should be retrieved first. The default offset value is 0 (first position in the database) and maximum is 200000. This maximum number is set to avoid performance issues. If you want to display more processes than defined in 'limit', you can create several requests with a different offset:
1. Create a request for the most recent items: GET /processes?limit=20&offset=0
2. On scroll/next page, create a second request: GET /processes?limit=20&offset=20
3. On scroll/next page, create a third request: GET /processes?limit=20&offset=40

limit.description: This is the maximum number of processes allowed in the response. The default value is 100 and maximum allowed is 2000. If you want to look at more processes than the defined limit, you must make more requests using the 'offset' parameter (see the example above).

order.description:
Allows to choose the order on which the processes will be returned - by descending or ascending createdAt date.
Default is descending.

subResult.description:
This field indicates a more detailed result for document reports. The available values are:
  • `clear`: All underlying verifications have passed.
  • `rejected`: The report has returned information that stops any further processing (poor quality image or an unsupported document).
  • `suspected`: The analysed document is suspected to be fraudulent.
  • `caution`: The report has returned information that needs to be evaluated.

ageValidation.description: Asserts whether the age calculated from the date of birth, is greater than or equal to the minimum acceptable age set at the account level. The standard threshold is 16 years old but you can get Signicat Support to change this for you. Available values are `clear` and `consider`.

policeRecord.description: Asserts whether the document has been identified as lost, stolen or otherwise compromised. The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

dataConsistency.description: Asserts whether data represented in multiple places in the document is consistent, for example, between MRZ and OCR extracted text on passports. Validated elements are date of expiry, document type, gender, date of birth, nationality, issuing country, document numbers, first name and last name.The available values  are `clear` and `consider`.

dataValidation.description: Asserts whether algorithmic validated elements are correct. For example, MRZ lines, document expiry date  and document numbers. The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

imageIntegrity.description: Asserts whether the document was of sufficient quality to verify. The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

dataComparison.description: Asserts whether the elements provided by the end-user match the information in the document, i.e. first name, last name, gender and date of birth. The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

faceComparison.description:
Asserts whether the face in the document matches the face in the selfie/video.
The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

compromisedDocument.description: Asserts whether the image of the document is found in Onfido's internal database of compromised documents. The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

result.description:
This field indicates the overall result of a report. The available values are:
  • `clear`: All underlying verifications have passed.
  • `consider`: The report has returned information that needs to be evaluated.
  • `unidentified`: This is returned if the applicant fails an identity check.

widowName.description: The widow name. (will only appear in the response if the field exists and is extracted by the provider)

restrictedLicence.description: True for limited/restricted driving licences, including learner's permits

visualAuthenticity.description:
Asserts whether visual (non-textual) elements are correct given the type of document, for example:
  • Is it a photo of a printed photo or of a digital screen (may be configured to map to caution)?
  • Has the document been digitally tampered, e.g. is the name altered?
  • Does the pictures of the person in the ID document show signs of tampering or alteration. In most cases, this will focus on the primary picture. It may also apply to the secondary and tertiary pictures when documents contain them.
  • Are security features expected in the ID document missing or wrong?
  • Does the document match the expected template for the document type and country?
  • Do the fonts in the document match the expected ones.?
The available values are `clear` and `consider`.

auditTrailVerificationCheckSucceeded.description: Checks if the audit trail image comes from the same session as the face scan (true or false).

replayCheckSucceeded.description: Checks that the face scan does not exist in the database.

faceScanLivenessCheckSucceeded.description: Checks if the liveness check succeeded.

sessionTokenCheckSucceeded.description: Checks if the session token is valid, and not expired.

error.description: Indicates whether an error occurred during the liveness verification (true) or not (false).

success.description: Indicates whether the liveness verification ended successfully (true) or not (false).

faceScanSecurityChecks.description: This object presents the results of the different security checks performed during the liveness scan.

matchLevel.description:
This is the match level between the face in the image and the face in the face map. The available values are (FAR = false acceptance rate):
  • 8 - 1/1,000,000 FAR
  • 7 - 1/500,000 FAR
  • 6 - 1/100,000 FAR
  • 5 - 1/10,000 FAR
  • 4 - 1/1,000 FAR
  • 3 - 1/500 FAR
  • 2 - 1/250 FAR
  • 1 - 1/100 FAR
  • 0 - Non-match
The possible values depend on the matching algorithm that was used:
  • facePortrait: 0 to 8
  • idPhoto:0 to 7
  • idPhotoLowQuality: 0 to 5

imageProcessing.description:
This field describes the quality of the image that was used in the matching.
The available values are:
  • `faceFound`: The face was found.
  • `invalidImage`: The image was not valid.
  • `faceDetectionIssue`: There was an issue detecting the face in the image.
  • `lowQuality`: The image has insufficient quality.

success.description: Indicates whether the matching was successful (true) or not (false).

error.description: Indicates whether an error occurred during the matching verification (true) or not (false).

attempts.description:
This is a list of all attempts at matching the face/document image with FaceTec's face map. It will contain minimum 1 attempt and maximum 3. Each attempt uses a different matching algorithm in the following order:
  • The first attempt uses the 'facePortrait' algorithm.
  • The second attempt uses the 'idPhoto' algorithm.
  • The third attempt uses the 'idPhotoLowQuality' algorithm.

matchLevel.description:
This is the match level between the face in the image and the face in the face map. The available values are (FAR = false acceptance rate):
  • 8 - 1/1,000,000 FAR
  • 7 - 1/500,000 FAR
  • 6 - 1/100,000 FAR

  • 4 - 1/1,000 FAR
  • 3 - 1/500 FAR

The possible values depend on the matching algorithm that was used:
  • facePortrait: 0 to 8
  • idPhoto:0 to 7

imageProcessing.description:

The available values are:
  • `faceFound`: The face was found.
  • `invalidImage`: The image was not valid.
  • `faceDetectionIssue`: There was an issue detecting the face in the image.
  • `lowQuality`: The image has insufficient quality.

success.description: Indicates whether the matching was successful (true) or not (false).

error.description: Indicates whether an error occurred during the matching verification (true) or not (false).

result.description:
This field indicates the overall result of a report. Possible values can be:
  • `clear`: All underlying verifications have passed.
  • `consider`: The report has returned information that needs to be evaluated
  • `unidentified`: This is returned if the applicant fails an identity check.

imageIntegrity.description:
Asserts whether the quality of the uploaded files and the content within are sufficient to do a face comparison, for example:
  • If the image of the picture is in colour (may be configured to be rejected if not).
  • If a single face of good enough quality is found in both the document image and the selfie/video.
  • If the selfie/video is not trustworthy, i.e. may have been digitally tampered from a fake webcam or from other dubious sources.
  • If the document image has good enough quality to perform a fraud inspection (for example, if parts of the document are not visible).
Possible values are `clear` and `consider`.

faceComparison.description:
Asserts whether the face in the document matches the face in the selfie/video.
Possible values are `clear` and `consider`.

visualAuthenticity.description:
Asserts whether the person in the selfie or live video is real (not a spoof) and alive (only done for live videos).
Possible values are `clear` and `consider`."

similarityScore.description:
This indicates how similar the two faces are. If the face matching algorithm fails to detect a face, the score property will not be present. The score only measures how similar the faces are, and does not assess the nature of the photo or video. If tampering (such as photos of printed photos or photos of digital screens) is detected, the end-user is rejected regardless of the facial similarity score. Possible values are `high`, `medium`, `low` or `veryLow`. Onfido originally returns a floating point number between 0 and 1 that expresses how similar the two faces are, where 1 is a perfect match. Assure normalizes that value into:
  • `high` if the score is > 0.75.
  • `medium` if the score is > 0.5 and <= 0.75.
  • `low` if the score is > 0.25 and <= 0.5.
  • `veryLow` if the score is <= 0.25.

breakdowns.description: Contains detailed information on each verification performed by Onfido regarding the facial similarity. For more info, please refer to https://documentation.onfido.com/guide/facial-similarity-reports/#photo-and-photo-fully-auto.

name.description: Name of the visual feature in the document.

found.description: Indicates if the feature was found in the document or not.

similarityScore.description:
The similarity score between the end-user's face and the photo in the ID. The value indicates the probability of the person in the selfie being the same as the person in the document image. Possible values are:
  • `veryLow`: Very unlikely the same person.
  • `low`: Probably not the same person.
  • `medium`: Probably the same person.
  • `high`: Very likely the same person.
To know more about what this score means for each provider, see the `providerSpecific` fields for each provider

livenessDetected.description: Ensures there is a living person in front of the camera through a facial expression (smile).

similarityScore.description: This is the biometric match level between the face of the person holding the document and the picture on it. Possible values are `high` (very likely the same person), `medium` (probably the same person), `low`(probably not) and `veryLow` (very unlikely). Default settings require `medium` as a minimum similarity level for the person to be able to complete the video identification. Other users will not be able to finish the process. If you want to change the default settings, please contact <support@signicat.com>.

features.description:
List of visual features extracted from the document.
Features mean the visual elements used in the ID document, like for example header1, header2, texts, bounding boxes, photo etc.
The list of features can vary from document to document, meaning that a feature (e.g. 'Header 1') may exist in one document but not in another document. This list will contain each feature's name, an indication on whether the feature was found or not and information about its position in the document.

dateOfExpiry.description: The expiry date of the document (YYYY-MM-DD). `VALID` if the date is in the future, `INVALID` otherwise.

isPartialRedaction.description: Possible values are `true`or `false`. true: At least one of the fields that were requested to be redacted were found (thus, were redacted). Check the value of `fullRedaction` to see if they all were redacted or not. false: None of the fields requested to be redacted were found (thus no field could be redacted).

isFullRedaction.description: Possible values are `true`or `false`. true: All fields that were requested to be redacted were found (thus, were successfully redacted). false: At least one of the fields that were requested to be redacted was not found (thus could not be redacted).

type.description:
The type of verification. Possible values are:
  • MRZ_FEATURE_COMPARISON: Retrieved the information from both the `mrz` and the text fields (features) from the document and will analyse and match their values.
  • MRZ_ONLY: Retrieved and analysed only the information from the `mrz` area (used with “passport” documents only).
  • FEATURES_ONLY: Retrieved and analysed only the information from the text fields (used with documents that do not have `mrz` and only have text fields).

status.description: The status of the verification. if "true", the verification was successful, if "false" at least one error was found.

permissionToDrive.description: Whether they are qualified for a passenger car, such as a “B” class in the UK

obtainmentDate.description: Date the class qualification was obtained

expiryDate.description: Date the class qualification expires, which may be different to doc expiry

processId.description: processId is obtained from the "Create process" response body.

provider.description: The ID of one of the supported providers.

processType.description: The process type created from the provider.

status.description: The current status of the the identity document verification process. For more information about what each status means, see the Get process page in the integration documentation.

depictions.description:
This field displays a list of available image types ("back", "front", "selfie", "portrait") representing the files stored in a process during its creation. This tells you which images you can get from a process. By knowing this you can avoid unnecessary calls to the Get image endpoint, trying to get images that do not exist in the process. Notes:
  • The field will only exist for processes with a final status.
  • If the process is finished and contains no images, the field will contain an empty array. Ex: "depictions": [].
  • All depictions of images that exist in the process will appear in this list, including images that might have been redacted by request (to which you will get an HTTP 403 UNAUTHORIZED if you try to Get Image, as usual)

finalResult.description:
The element in this area is only present if the verification was accepted.
The elements in this node depend on the document/provider/processType that was used in the process creation.

providerSpecific.description: Contains information retrieved by the provider. Inner elements will differ depending on the provider

redactionRequested.description: This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider. The flag is set to true if the redaction was requested.

statusReasons.description:
Warning: This is a beta feature.
This field is only available for signicatpictureid provider.
It contains a list of reasons of why a process status had an inconclusive or rejected state.

documentVerification.description:
This field only applies to the 'onfido' provider.
It contains information from Onfido's document verification.

facialSimilarity.description:
This field only applies to the 'onfido' provider.
Contains information from Onfido's facial similarity verification.

verification.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It contains information about the type of verification that was done by Signicat Paper. Either it verified both the `mrz` area and the textual fields of the document or it was able to only check one of those.

features.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It contains values extracted from the textual fields in the document.

mrz.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It contains the information that was retrieved from MRZ area in the document.

mrzChecksum.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It contains information about checksum evaluations performed over the checkdigits in the `mrz`. Possible values are:
  • VALID: The checksum evaluation is valid;
  • INVALID: The checksum evaluation is not valid.

mrzVerification.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It contains information about the validity of some of the `mrz` fields in the document. Possible values are:
  • VALID: The value is a valid date.
  • PARSED: The value is not a valid date.
  • FOUND: The value was found but it cannot be parsed as a date (meaning it will also not be a valid date).
  • MISSING: The value was not found.

mrzFeatureMatching.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It provides matching results from comparing the values with the `features` field with the corresponding values in the `mrz` feature fields (for example `lastName`, `firstName`, `dateOfBirth` etc.). Possible values are:
  • FULL_MATCH: Used for both date and text fields. The value in the feature field matches completely the value in `mrz`.
  • LEVENSHTEIN1, LEVENSHTEIN2, LEVENSHTEIN3, LEVENSHTEIN4: Used for both date and text fields. The value in the feature field differs from the value in `mrz` from 1 to 4 characters.
  • VALID: The difference is greater than LEVENSHTEIN4 but the value in the feature field is still a valid date.
  • PARSED: Used for date fields only. The difference is greater than LEVENSHTEIN4 but the value in the field is not a valid date.
  • FOUND: For text fields, the value was found in the field but it differs more than 5 degrees from the value in `mrz`. For date fields, the value was found in the field but it cannot be parsed as a date (meaning it will also not be a valid date).
  • MISSING: The value was not found in any field.

featuresVerification.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
This is only presented when the document has no MRZ (ex: Danish health card))
Provides information if the field was found or not in the document.

redactionResult.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
It contains the redaction result when redaction is requested for one or more fields in an identity document. The redaction result will contain "partial" and "full" boolean values.

Notes about redaction:
  • This information only exists if `redact` is sent in the request body.
  • The redaction results do not have any impact on the process' final status. The process status indicates the success of the verification and redaction is a parallel feature.
For more details, see the Redaction section in the integration documentation.

errors.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpaper' provider.
List of errors found by SignicatPaper during the verification.

ocr.description:
This field only applies to the 'eid' provider.
It contains information obtained from Signicat VideoID's OCR analysis of the document during the video identification.

manualApproval.description:
This field only applies when the provider is 'signicatvideoid' and `processType` is `high`.
It contains the data that was extracted by ElectronicID during the video identification and after it was manually reviewed by an agent. The sub-fields are similar to the ones you find in `ocr` but may have been improved.

consolidatedIdentityData.description:
This field only applies to the 'readid' provider.
It contains information from the Read ID consolidated data.

documentContent.description:
This field only applies to the 'readid' provider.
It contains some more information present in the identity document.

iproovSession.description:
This field only applies to the 'readid' provider.
It contains information from the iProov liveness session.

liveness.description:
This field only applies to the 'facetec' provider.
This object contains information from FaceTec's liveness verification.

matching.description:
This field only applies to the 'facetec' provider.
This object contains information from FaceTec's matching verification.

document.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpictureid' provider.
It contains information from Signicat Picture ID document verification.

selfie.description:
This field only applies to the 'signicatpictureid' provider.
Contains information from Signicat Picture ID facial similarity verification.

chipVerification.description: This is the chip verification status. It verifies that the contents in the chip is authentic. Values can be either `SUCCEEDED` or `FAILED`. Assure API uses the value from this field in combination with ChipCloneDetection to map the final status of the process. For more details about the final status mapping, see the ReadID results section in the general documentation.

chipCloneDetection.description: This is the chip clone detection status. It verifies if the actual chip is used (and not a clone). Values can be either `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED` or `NOT_AVAILABLE`. Assure API uses the value from this field in combination with ChipVerification to map the final status of the process. For more details about the final status mapping, see the ReadID results section in the general documentation.
